understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through each KA59 level by moving/click-selecting pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time; when all goals are covered simultaneously the level reports WIN, and completing the final level wins the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently controlled selectable is identified by player.id/position and shown as 'P' in the semantic grid, while other selectables are 'S'. MOVE actions attempt to shift the selected piece by one 3-pixel grid cell in the chosen direction (x right/left, y down/up). If a pushable block is directly adjacent in the move direction, the piece can push it so both shift, but movement is blocked if a wall/boundary or an unpushable configuration prevents the shift. Walls '#' act as obstacles with potentially non-uniform blocking behavior, and the movement_*_blocked flags are only hints about adjacency, not guarantees of failure. CLICK switches control to another selectable at the exact pixel coordinates given (same coordinate system as player.position and objects positions).
